title: Travel Basics: Hotels, Taxis, and Simple Directions
objectives: Use essential travel phrases for hotels, taxis, bags, and nearby places.
Understand Carlos in short guided exchanges.
Use the lesson language in one realistic conversation.
objective: Use essential travel phrases for hotels, taxis, bags, and nearby places.

vocabulary.tip: Carlos tip: practice ??D?nde est?...?? in a short real sentence, not as an isolated word.
vocabulary.exampleSpanish: Carlos usa ?D?nde est?...? en una frase.
vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Carlos uses Where is...? in a sentence.

grammar.explanation: Use est? to say where something is. Use ?D?nde est?...? to ask where a place or thing is.

examples.spanish: La estaci?n est? a la derecha.
examples.english: The station is on the right.

grammar.practicePrompt: Say one original sentence using today?s pattern with Carlos.

lines.english: I say it slowly and respectfully.

culture.title: Travel Basics in Real Life
culture.text: In travel situations, short polite phrases are better than perfect grammar. Start with disculpe to get attention politely.

cultureNotes: In travel situations, short polite phrases are better than perfect grammar. Start with disculpe to get attention politely.

nativeSpeech.title: How Carlos would say it naturally
notes: ??Me puede ayudar?? is a natural help phrase.
?Todo recto? means straight ahead in many direction conversations.

commonMistakes.correction: Salida can mean exit or departure.
commonMistakes.explanation: Context matters in stations and airports.

realLifeMission.title: Real Life Mission
realLifeMission.mission: Ask Carlos for a taxi, say where your hotel is, and ask where the bathroom is.
successCriteria: Use at least three lesson vocabulary items.
Answer one follow-up question from Carlos.

review.summary: You practiced travel basics for everyday Spanish.
canDo: Answer Carlos with simple complete sentences.
Use the Real Life Mission in a practical situation.
